item.title: Is it worse to be a wagie or a NEET? — 145 replies — 580.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Is it worse to be a wagie or a NEET?

Open thread • Replies: 145 • Updated: 2026-04-29 03:57 UTC

OP

Is it worse to be a wagie or a NEET?

Early replies (first 3)

62158601
>>62158557
Neet
Wagie can get some money to go out and have fun, and socialize.
might not be your cup of tea, until you making out with a 8/10 girl who you just said hi to, 2 minutes ago

62158623
>>62158601
>might not be your cup of tea, until you making out with a 8/10 girl who you just said hi to, 2 minutes ago

while waging at walmart is impossible

62158626
>>62158557
I prefer to be a neet, but ofc neither situation is perfect. Now if i was getting pussy then we'd be talking

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62165024
Both sides in this thread are coping a bit.
NEET feels like freedom at first but for most people it turns into stagnation unless you're extremely self-directed. You lose structure, your sense of time gets weird, and eventually nothing feels that rewarding.
Wagie life sucks in a different way (routine, low autonomy, sometimes humiliation) but it gives you money, exposure to people, and at least some structure.
NEET + no direction = decay
Wagie + no progression = slow trap
NEET + discipline (learning, building, training) = rare but viable
Wagie + skill-building / saving = actually opens options over time
Most people in this thread are arguing about comfort vs discomfort, not outcomes.
Also: if you're the type who does nothing with your free time while working you're not suddenly going to become productive as a NEET. It usually gets worse, not better.
If you care about long-term quality of life, the question is: are you gaining leverage (skills, money, options) or just maintaining your current state?
Everything else is just preference and coping.

62165089
>>62158557
>wagie or a NEET
wagie because you can find yourself surround by negative people for a while
but they're both the worst with a bad mindset desu

62165090
>>62164928
False. Because most people in society are wagies, socializing happens during the same 5pm-2am time frame. It's why being a neet sucks because none of your friends are available as they are working and you can only hangout with them when they are done work which defeats the whole point of you not working.
>>62165024
This.

62165202
>>62165024
Literally ChatGPT written. Also, you aren’t developing fucking skills at your call center job or flipping beef patties. None of those open any options for you and are time sink. Jobs also don’t change your sense of time, all a job does is make you adhere to a schedule and some schedules are fucked up so it’s the same as NEETING. The wealth is concentrated at the top and most of the rich today inherited wealth they did NOT work for it AT ALL. Get that thru your fucking head. You are labor and no laborer ends up like Trump or Musk. Most labor ends up back broken and tossed aside later in life. 50% of all baby boomers are broke and destitute that’s proof enough waging isn’t the correct path

62165417
>>62158557
Waging is worse. The problem with waging is that most jobs are full time with a pitiful allotment of days off.
Like seriously, I get 4 weeks of personal leave a year. That amounts to a couple of 2 week holidays in a year, just fucking nothing. Its such a small amount of time I cant even stand to use it because im psychologically cucked knowing im going to be counting down those days before i have to get back to work again. And then when i get back to work, I will have fewer days left in the bank and the despair will set in further.
Being a neet was being a loser with no hope but at least you can put yourself in a state of mild contentment through trivial entertainment.
Being a wagie is a state of joylessness, boredom and stress. And then you're so time poor you cant enjoy anything in between so the money is irrelevant.
Waging is below me as a human being. I can work, I can feel fulfilled overcoming challenges and building towards things. But i can not, and will not hold up the merits of doing a constant stream of unending tasks 8 hours a day, 5 days a week. There is not a life for a conscious human.

62165497
>>62158557
Wagie. Dealing with normgroids sucks donkey penis.

62165528
The unfortunate truth is that being a NEET is basically the ultimate way of fucking yourself over, unless big inheritance is guaranteed. There's no way of putting into words how fucked a ~30 year old NEET is whose parents are getting up there in age and aren't wealthy. Even if you were in your early twenties, if you've been a NEET for even just a few years you have fucked yourself over massively.

It might not seem that way when you're sitting in your comfy room with electricity and food and bills getting paid for by someone else, but one day, that someone else will be gone. The lights will dim, that person won't be there to give you food or buy you things anymore, and there will be silence. And you'll be alone. And all those years you spent thinking "haha, those wagies are such cucks!" and "idc what happens to me I'll just rope doomer patrick bateman perfect girl edit slowed extended" won't have meant anything. Reality will hit you like the Malaysian Airlines flight 370 Boeing 777 hit the Indian Ocean doing a full speed nosedive. You will realize that you have lived over 1/4th of your life and you haven't begun a career and you have nothing to show for yourself, meanwhile young twenty something are working towards their masters degrees or already working salary jobs. And you'll realize something absolutely catastrophic: you CAN'T rope. You can't do it. There really is no escape, you are only now realizing that you can't bring yourself to do it. You don't have to die to go to hell, you just have to live your life as a NEET.

62165968
>>62165528
>tl:dr wageoid seething
Kys retard.

62166001
>>62165528
>muh show for yourself normoid perspective
Kek. That doesn't matter. The big problem is that there's only so much good anime to watch and games to play. You run out of quality entertainment VERY quickly, and you'll start losing your mind playing MOBA trash or watching moeshit, or being a prominent member of a niche online community, and your days will be filled with nostalgia about enjoying things from years and years ago, now that's a fate worse than death.

Work alone most of the time. Micromanaging almost non existent. Flexible work schedule. Just name some of the pros.

Sure there are cons to it as well like anything else but some of those are non issues (you might sweat from time to time…). Guys who have been here as long or longer than me aren’t using walkers to get around. We’re all perfectly fit. There are tools for every job. Good my brother. The first step to enlightenment is to admit and accept that you're tired. You first need to see your darkness in order for you to bring the light. Those who seek the light have experienced immense darkness. Brother, this is a moment of rejoice. Accept your pain, let it go, move on and forgive yourself or the people who have cause it. But remember brother the world is simply an illusion and by that I mean your own perception of reality. It's all inside you and not in the outside world. A river is not beautiful or magnificent, that's your illusion of it. A river is simply water that is moving, nothing more, nothing less. The illusion feels real because just like when you dream the pain feels real, the fear feels real, the sorrow feels real. Once you wake up from the dream you realize that nothing was real, that it was just an illusion. But it is true that when you were in the dream the pain felt real. So, once we see the illusion for what it is it'll start dissolving and once it dissolves that's then the reason to work inside the world without attachments and to help those who are asleep to wake up.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),

Once all of those dissolve it'll uncover enlightenment. So, enlightenment isn't an achievement or something you do, it's literally that you stop doing. It's the fact that the doer stops. The watcher and the watched go away. The only thing that remains is the watching.

They intellectualize, "There may be hope for other people, but there's no hope for me.". Such a potent intellectual defense mechanism. And what are they defending against? The belief of hope. Because hope hurts. If there's zero chance, there's zero need for effort. See, human beings are really resilient. We can live through genocide and holocaust and all kinds of stuff. We can live through all kinds of terrible things, terrible economic times. We're survivors. But don't give us hope, because if we can hope, we can hurt.

Having hope also exposes you to hurt. And hurt is a normal part of life. If you're hurting that means you're human. It's just. Are you going to hurt consciously and change something or are you going to hurt unconsciously and keep on hurting due to the same thing for the rest of your life?

You maybe haven't "You go this" at the moment. You may feel powerless. You may feel down. And at the same time you'll slowly and gradually regain your power. You'll unburden the weight. Sooner or later you'll feel so light you'll start flying. I really believe that, even though it may at the current moment not feel like it. Even though you might feel like the current moment is never ending trust me, it'll all change and reshape into a healthier future. You got this fren who's reading this.

Your dear Vishnu Kumar wishes you are peaceful day. May Vishnu of the mindchain be with and all around you.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
